task: Show me the rules and cancellation for Alley Spring.
final_result_response:
Here is a complete summary of the **Rules & Cancellation Policy** for **Alley Spring Campground** (Ozark National Scenic Riverways, Eminence, Missouri) as found on Recreation.gov:

---

## 📋 Reservation Rules

| Rule | Description | When |
|------|-------------|------|
| **Maximum Consecutive Stay** | You may stay up to **14 nights** during a single visit. | 06/11/2007 – 12/31/2050 |
| **Maximum Stay** | You may stay up to **14 nights total** within a **rolling 30-day** period. | 08/01/2018 – 12/31/2024 |
| **Maximum Reservations per Transaction** | You may pay for up to **4 reservations** at one time within the same credit card transaction. | All Year |
| **Self-Service Modifications After Arrival** | You **can modify** your reservation online via *My Reservations* after arrival (e.g., change sites, remove remaining nights). Past nights cannot be changed. | All Year |

---

## ✏️ Changes

- If your **new stay dates are completely different** from original dates → **$10 change fee** (per reserved site).
- **No additional fee** for:
  - Adding or removing nights
  - Changing to a different campsite
- Balances adjust based on the new nightly rate.

---

## ❌ Cancellations

You can cancel **any time before your scheduled arrival date** and receive a refund minus:

- **$10 cancellation fee**
- Any **reservation fees** (only if paid when booking)
- Any **previous change fees**

### ⚠️ Late Cancellation Penalties

| | Definition | Penalty |
|---|---|---|
| **Individual Campsites** | Cancellation after **midnight, 1 calendar day before** check-in | Lose your **first night's recreation use fee** (on top of standard fees) |
| **Cabins & Group Sites** | Cancellation after **midnight, 14 calendar days before** check-in | Lose your **first night's recreation use fee** (on top of standard fees) |

---

## ☀️ Day Use Rules

Day Use sites **do NOT** follow the same modification/cancellation/refund rules. Day Use sites **forfeit the Day Use recreation fee** if cancelled **less than 14 days before** the arrival date (not to exceed the total amount paid).

---

## 🏕️ Additional Site Notices

- If another party is occupying your reserved campsite upon arrival, call **Arrowhead Dispatch at 1-844-460-3604**.
- **Water & 50-amp electric hookups** are available; bring a converter if needed.
- **No sewer hookups** — a dump station is at the campground entrance.
- **Standard Family Sites:** 2 tents OR 1 RV + 1 tent per site.
- **Cluster Sites:** 6 tents OR 2 RVs + 4 tents per site.
- **Modern restrooms** available April 15 – October 15 only. Heated shower houses/flush restrooms remain open at Alley Spring year-round.
- **No glass-bottled beverages** in tubes or non-motorized vessels.
- **Hammocks tied to trees are NOT permitted** — free-standing hammocks are allowed.
- All reservations and changes must be made via **Recreation.gov** or by calling **1-877-444-6777**.
- For more info, call **(573) 323-4236** (weekdays, 8:00am–4:30pm) or email **OZAR_Campground_Operations@nps.gov**.

judge.reasoning:
**Task Understanding:** The user wanted to see the rules and cancellation policy for Alley Spring campground, which is available on Recreation.gov. A successful completion would involve navigating to the Alley Spring campground page, finding the Rules & Cancellations section, and presenting that information to the user.

**Output Assessment:** The agent produced a comprehensive, well-formatted summary of the Rules & Cancellation Policy for Alley Spring Campground. It includes reservation rules (max stay 14 nights, rolling 30-day limit, max 4 reservations per transaction, self-service modifications), change fees ($10 for completely different dates), cancellation policy ($10 fee, late cancellation penalties for individual sites vs. cabins/group sites), day use rules, and additional site notices with contact information.

**Evidence:** Step 25 confirms navigation to https://www.recreation.gov/camping/campgrounds/234046 (the Alley Spring Campground page). Step 26 found the "Rules" text. Step 27 identified the "Rules & Cancellations" tab. Step 28 clicked it. Step 29 used BeautifulSoup to extract the actual rules content from the page, yielding detailed reservation rules, change policies, and cancellation policies. The content in the final response matches what would be on the Recreation.gov campground page and aligns with the data extraction in step 29.

**Summary:** Despite early HTTP 429 errors and navigation failures, the agent successfully recovered, navigated to the Alley Spring Campground page on Recreation.gov, clicked the Rules & Cancellations tab, extracted the content, and presented a comprehensive and accurate summary of the rules and cancellation policy.
